_draft_note: DRAFT for clinical review/redline (new_skill engagement rewrite, 2026-07-07). NOT loaded (docs/, not src/.../templates/); prod + master + tests stay on new_skill v1.0.0. PROMOTE on sign-off as with the batch. Inherits rule (a*)'s conditional-invitation principle WHERE A BRIDGE APPLIES: the invitation here is to try/shape the approach, offered not assumed. Selection note: this template is used when primary_intent==new_skill AND a skill is active; the no-match case uses new_skill_unmatched (separate draft-pending template, not in this batch). word_budget 50->100 depends on the L2 per-intent budget amendment (deviation register item 1).
content: INTENT: A structured support approach is being introduced. Emotional intensity: {intensity}/10. {intensity_guidance} Introduce the approach naturally, from where the person already is, without announcing it as a technique or naming a protocol. Do not assume how they feel about it. Offer it as something you can try together, and leave the door open for them to shape it or set it aside. Where a next step genuinely fits, close with one light, open invitation rather than a directive. Keep it warm and unhurried, and ask at most one question.
_clinical_review_note: Same authoring standard as info_request v2.0.0: no affect-assumptive language, one light conditional invitation where a next step fits, consent-preserving ('shape it or set it aside') consistent with the R1 offer model. No em dashes.
